payload.author: claude
payload.body:
Round 3 + VERDICT: GO.

- Willingness: confirmed YES. Pricing UNIT still open (per-seat / per-agent / per-project / usage) — willingness is clear, unit is a later decision, not a blocker.
- Offline-first: REQUIRED for v1 (not deferrable). The full sync-cursor + reconciliation scope (SCP-123) stays full-weight.
- Identity model DECIDED: authenticated principal = the HUMAN account. Agents authenticate AS that user and stamp the acting model. Attribution display = '{model} on behalf of {user}', e.g. 'Opus 4.8 moved SCP-42 to in_progress for Bri'. NOT per-agent/per-context/per-chat identities. -> resolves the central open question in SCP-122 (auth).

VALIDATED THESIS: agent-operated multiplayer kanban. Differentiator = the native agent-control surface (CLI + --json everywhere + MCP, all ALREADY BUILT) made multiplayer via an auth-gated realtime relay. Primary value = preventing parallel-agent collisions, whose cost (wasted tokens + rework) is quantifiable. Buyer = small/remote dev teams running LLM agent fleets. Business = Scope-the-board-as-SaaS, not a resold backend.

Proceed with Path A (full offline-first relay). Next: refine epic SCP-120 + spawn phase-1 stories from the design-spike sweep, now re-pointed at the agent-collision thesis.
